category_title: Students with reactive behaviors and work refusal
minimal_context: The student is using a drawing software tool during class time to create a digital picture about fairness and honesty.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time to finish your designated work. Please use the drawing software tool to create a digital picture depicting your ideas about fairness and honesty.

STUDENT: I don't want to do this. I should be out playing handball right now instead of doing this.

TUTOR: What do you think you could draw?

STUDENT: Nothing. I don't care about making a visual representation of fair play or 'no cheating' in rugby league.

TUTOR: Please keep trying to complete the normal classroom work.

STUDENT: No. I'm not doing it. I refuse to do any more work today.

options.A: Blur boundaries between subjects to focus on integrated themes and co-construct a shared sense of morality.
options.B: Check the student's progress on the software every Sunday night to identify mistakes and struggling topics.
options.C: Engage the student in drawing, painting, water coloring, and pottery making during class time.
options.D: Make oneself available without forcing interaction, first sitting near the student, speaking quietly, and gently touching their shoulder.
